name: Welcome Pack
shortDescription: A balanced first order with everyday staples, a premium cut, and enough variety to get close to free delivery.
strategySignals: 7+ item first baskets retain 62.3% vs 29.6% for one-item baskets
Multi-category first baskets retain +9.8pp
Target AOV above the $227 retention inflection

curationSlots.label: Chicken anchor
curationSlots.categoryRule: One retail chicken protein, preferably cutlets or 8-piece cut-up; avoid institutional bulk.

curationSlots.label: Premium second category
curationSlots.categoryRule: One lamb or veal cut that nudges the customer beyond a single-category basket.

curationSlots.label: Weeknight staple
curationSlots.categoryRule: One ground beef or burger item that makes the basket practical.

curationSlots.label: Heritage or prepared marker
curationSlots.categoryRule: One South African or heat-and-serve item that makes GP feel distinct.

name: Chicken Lamb Veal Starter
shortDescription: A balanced starter set with chicken, lamb, veal, and ground beef for a few different meals.
strategySignals: Veal +12.7pp, chicken +8.9pp, lamb +6.5pp vs baseline in first baskets

name: Free Delivery First Order Builder
shortDescription: A practical way to build a full first order around local free delivery, with dinners that make sense together.
strategySignals: AOV above $227 moves retention into the stronger Q3 band
Local free-delivery threshold is a natural merchandising goal

name: First Shabbos Dinner Starter
shortDescription: A first GP Shabbos order with chicken, brisket, lamb, and a ready side for the table.
strategySignals: Avoid prepared-only first baskets; steer toward raw-protein pantry behavior

name: First-Time Premium Sampler
shortDescription: A premium sampler with steakhouse cuts, organic chicken, lamb, and veal for customers who want to try the range.
strategySignals: Organic chicken breasts and oyster steak showed 85.7% first-basket retention in the available sample

name: First-Time Family Freezer Starter
shortDescription: Retail-pack freezer basics that make the first order useful for more than one meal.
strategySignals: Moves first orders toward 4+ item multi-category baskets without relying on discounting

name: Atlanta Local Favorites
shortDescription: A local-delivery friendly set for Atlanta families who want a complete week of kosher staples.
strategySignals: Local customers are the highest-frequency base; help them build bigger repeatable baskets

name: National Shipping Steakhouse Pack
shortDescription: A cold-chain friendly mix of steaks, lamb, brisket, and biltong for a larger shipped order.
strategySignals: National shipping needs denser, higher-dollar baskets to protect contribution margin

name: Chicken Beef Lamb Reorder Pack
shortDescription: Restock across the butcher case with chicken, beef, lamb, and an easy prepared item for a fuller next order.
strategySignals: Cross-sell unpurchased categories after first order, especially if the first basket was single-category

name: Pantry Builder: Chicken, Beef, Lamb
shortDescription: A simple butcher-case stock-up: chicken, beef, lamb, and a flexible extra for the freezer.
strategySignals: Multi-category baskets retain better than single-category baskets

name: Shabbos Dinner Made Easy
shortDescription: A full-table Shabbos set with a main, a backup main, and easy sides.
strategySignals: Occasion-driven baskets reduce decision load and naturally expand item count

name: Shabbos Chicken Dinner
shortDescription: Chicken choices for a classic Friday night table, with enough variety for different eaters.
strategySignals: Chicken is the highest-volume positive retention category

name: Shabbos Roast and Sides
shortDescription: A roast-centered Shabbos order with prepared sides to cut down the Friday workload.
strategySignals: Premium raw-protein buyers behave more like pantry builders than event samplers

name: Shabbos Brisket Table
shortDescription: Brisket at the center, with chicken, sides, and dessert to turn it into a complete order.
strategySignals: Brisket is a classic high-intent kosher purchase; use it to build a complete basket

name: Shabbos Slow Cooker Cholent
shortDescription: Everything the meat side of a serious cholent needs: meat, bones, fat, kishke, and ground beef.
strategySignals: Current PDP hardcoded basket showed cholent intent; promote it as a real editable collection

name: Shabbos Lunch Deli and Salmon
shortDescription: A no-cook Shabbos lunch helper built around smoked salmon and deli-friendly proteins.
strategySignals: Convenience support works best when attached to a broader basket, not as a one-item sampler

name: Shabbos Kids Favorites
shortDescription: Familiar proteins for families feeding kids without turning the whole order into processed food.
strategySignals: Use familiar products to support, not replace, a balanced protein basket

name: Shabbos Guest Table
shortDescription: A larger Shabbos table order for guests: chicken, brisket, lamb, and easy sides.
strategySignals: Occasion baskets naturally justify higher item count and AOV without discount framing

name: Shabbos Heat and Serve Backup
shortDescription: A backup set of prepared mains and sides for the week when cooking time disappears.
strategySignals: Prepared items are strongest as support for a broader relationship, not the only first-basket signal

name: Motzei Shabbos Grill
shortDescription: Quick grillable proteins for the night after Shabbos or a Sunday family cookout.
strategySignals: Grilling occasion creates a natural basket around burgers, sausage, steak, and wings

name: Weeknight Chicken Rotation
shortDescription: A week of chicken formats so families can reorder without rebuilding the cart from scratch.
strategySignals: Chicken is the best high-volume first-basket retention signal

name: Weeknight Cutlets and Stir Fry
shortDescription: Raw cutlets plus a stir-fry kit for customers who want one cooked-now and one cooked-later meal.
strategySignals: Pair convenience with raw protein to avoid prepared-only basket behavior

name: Weeknight Ground Beef Plan
shortDescription: A ground-beef centered order for burgers, meatballs, tacos, and easy dinners.
strategySignals: Ground beef is a basket utility product; cross-sell into premium cuts rather than treating it as the whole order

name: Weeknight Heat and Serve
shortDescription: Prepared mains and sides for nights when the alternative is skipping dinner.
strategySignals: Convenience matters, but prepared-only acquisition should be watched for retention drag

name: Weeknight Low Prep Family
shortDescription: A low-prep cart that still includes real proteins and not only prepared sides.
strategySignals: Use ease as the hook while keeping the basket protein-led

name: Soup and Bones Weeknight
shortDescription: Chicken bones, necks, and meaty beef additions for soup, broth, and slow cooking.
strategySignals: Functional cooking collections help customers find the less obvious cuts

name: Freezer Basics
shortDescription: Simple retail-pack staples that make the freezer useful without turning the cart into wholesale bulk.
strategySignals: Repeatable staples support second-order behavior

name: High-Protein Lunch Prep
shortDescription: Proteins that work for weekday lunches, salads, wraps, bowls, and fast reheats.
strategySignals: Expands beyond dinner-only merchandising

name: Comfort Dinner
shortDescription: Slow-cook and prepared comfort items for cold weeks, busy weeks, and family dinners.
strategySignals: Comfort-food intent can support higher AOV when paired with raw braising cuts

name: Prepared Sides Add-On
shortDescription: Round out a protein order with heat-and-serve sides and desserts for the table.
strategySignals: Designed for cart upsell, not acquisition; avoids making sides the first-order anchor

name: Grill Master Pack
shortDescription: Steak, burgers, sausage, and wings for a complete kosher grill session.
strategySignals: LLM basket candidate: Grill Master Pack; strong occasion clarity

name: Steak Night
shortDescription: A straightforward steak-night order with one premium cut and one more accessible cut.
strategySignals: Premium raw proteins are better retention signals than institutional/event bulk

name: Burger and Boerewors Grill
shortDescription: Burgers plus South African-style sausage for the most GP-specific grill basket.
strategySignals: Boerewors is a differentiated GP hook; cross-sell it with broader basket builders

name: Wings and Things
shortDescription: Retail-pack wings with a few easy add-ons for a game day, grill day, or Sunday crowd.
strategySignals: Crowd occasion helps increase quantity while avoiding institutional bulk as default

name: Premium Grill
shortDescription: Premium grill cuts for customers who want a smaller, better basket.
strategySignals: High $/lb baskets protect shipping economics

name: Summer Family Cookout
shortDescription: A family cookout cart: burgers, wings, sausage, and one steak option.
strategySignals: Seasonal grilling provides a non-holiday occasion for basket expansion

name: Tailgate Pack
shortDescription: Easy crowd proteins for game day without defaulting to commodity bulk.
strategySignals: Event baskets should still preserve GP quality and item transparency

name: Lamb on the Grill
shortDescription: Lamb chops, rack, and ground lamb for customers already showing premium lamb intent.
strategySignals: Lamb first-basket category retained +6.5pp above baseline

name: South African Heritage
shortDescription: The products that make Grillers Pride feel different: boerewors-style sausage, biltong, burgers, and grillable cuts.
strategySignals: Specialty products differentiate GP, but should be cross-sold into broader baskets

name: Boerewors and Biltong Discovery
shortDescription: A small but clear discovery set for South African specialty buyers, with a practical protein add.
strategySignals: Biltong alone is not a retention bet; attach it to broader cooking behavior

name: Heritage Grill and Snacks
shortDescription: A South African-flavored grill order with biltong as the cart-finishing snack.
strategySignals: Specialty adds should increase distinctiveness and AOV without becoming opaque bundles

name: Specialty Snack Drawer
shortDescription: Small specialty add-ons for customers already building a bigger order.
strategySignals: Cart-stage add-on designed for AOV, not first-basket acquisition

name: Heritage First Order Cross-Sell
shortDescription: For specialty-curious first-time buyers: the GP heritage item plus real dinner proteins.
strategySignals: Specialty-only first baskets are too sparse to trust; cross-sell into raw proteins

name: Premium Carnivore
shortDescription: A premium meat-lover's box: steak, lamb rack, brisket, biltong, and a practical grill add.
strategySignals: Round 4 recommendation: high-margin premium bundle validates curation without shipping pain

curationSlots.label: Premium steak
curationSlots.categoryRule: Ribeye, strip, cowboy, filet, hanger, or skirt; avoid cases and institutional bulk.

curationSlots.label: Premium lamb
curationSlots.categoryRule: Whole rack or first-cut lamb chops when available.

curationSlots.label: Classic braise
curationSlots.categoryRule: Brisket or short ribs in retail-pack quantity.

name: Steakhouse at Home
shortDescription: A steakhouse-style order with steak, lamb or veal, and one prepared side.
strategySignals: Premium occasion buying is a better fit for transparent collections than opaque discount bundles

name: Lamb Lover's Selection
shortDescription: A lamb-focused collection for customers who already know they want something special.
strategySignals: LLM bundle candidate: Lamb Lover's Selection; lamb buyers are high-AOV customers

name: Veal Sunday Roast
shortDescription: A veal-centered Sunday or Yom Tov roast order with braising cuts and a ready side.
strategySignals: Veal first-basket category retained +12.7pp above baseline, though smaller sample

name: Brisket and Short Rib Braise
shortDescription: A slow-cook beef collection for customers browsing brisket, short ribs, shank, or stew cuts.
strategySignals: Braising intent naturally supports multi-item, high-AOV carts

name: Rosh Hashanah Table
shortDescription: A festive Rosh Hashanah meat order with brisket, chicken, lamb, and sweet sides.
strategySignals: September/Rosh Hashanah is a major demand peak; holiday baskets reduce planning anxiety

curationSlots.label: Brisket anchor
curationSlots.categoryRule: First-cut brisket or equivalent holiday brisket portion, retail size.

curationSlots.label: Chicken main
curationSlots.categoryRule: Whole chicken or 8-piece cut-up depending on inventory.

curationSlots.label: Sweet holiday support
curationSlots.categoryRule: Tzimmes, honey carrots, apple dessert, or equivalent seasonal side.

name: Passover Early Order Backbone
shortDescription: The Passover backbone: brisket, chicken, lamb or veal, soup pieces, and the prepared items that sell out first.
strategySignals: Passover drives the largest baskets and strongest cohort retention; early ordering prevents stockouts

curationSlots.label: Seder beef
curationSlots.categoryRule: First-cut brisket or short rib braise cut, retail-size, KFP when applicable.

curationSlots.label: Chicken volume
curationSlots.categoryRule: Two chicken mains, preferably 8-piece cut-up or whole chicken, KFP when applicable.

curationSlots.label: Soup and ground
curationSlots.categoryRule: Chicken bones/necks plus ground beef for soup and fillings.
